title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded snapshot navigation history and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 2172 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate bounded successful-view snapshots and failure-safe back behavior; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate navigation sequences and verify cursor, stack, boundary, idempotence, and failure invariants across changed page sizes.

steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Pokedex Go History Property Tests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 9523 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate bounded successful-view snapshots and failure-safe back behavior; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate navigation sequences and verify cursor, stack, boundary, idempotence, and failure invariants across changed page sizes.

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go History Property….
steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Go History Property Tests through the concrete bounded snapshot navigation history case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go History Property…, keep Pokedex Go History Property Tests connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate navigation sequences and verify cursor, stack, boundary, idempotence, and failure invariants across changed page sizes.

hints: For Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go History Property…, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Pokedex Go History Property Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go History Property…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 9523 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate bounded successful-view snapshots and failure-safe back behavior; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate navigation sequences and verify cursor, stack, boundary, idempotence, and failure invariants across changed page sizes.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go History Property…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A few manually chosen navigation sequences cover every history defect.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: pokedex-go-history-property-tests-9btklp.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: pokedex-go-history-property-tests-9btklp(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_pokedex_go_history_property_tests_9btklp\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?successfulViews < 3)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?historyEntries < 1 \|\| historyEntries > successfulViews)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?failedPushes > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?capacity < historyEntries)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using generate and navigation plus specific case evidence.
